These are busy days With the
Young. Women's Christian associa
ton. Every department has all
that 1t can take care. OF, as the
membership of 1900 furnishes an
abundance of work. Miss Frances
Gage, northwest territorial inspec
tor, who was here Saturday, was
much pleased at the prosperous
condition of the association.


With an incorporated capital oil
$4,000 the Guernsey-Newton Co
has been organized to conduct q
general brokerage business in real
estate and investment securities
The promoters are o. E. Guernsey
H. w. Newton, w. H. Mariner, A
M. Riley and Fred J. Dibble.


The Inland Empire system will
erect new bridge across the Spo
kane river at Spokane Bridge. The
contract has been awarded and the
material is being assembled on the
ground.


The protest Of the adjacent prop
erty owner sto the erection of &

ton has been rejected by the board
of public works and the "permit is
sued to build the barn. The build
ing will comply with sanitary re
Quirements and will house Is heat

The Chicago, Milwaukee & Ppge'
Sound railroad must be conten,
with the verdict of $4,000 award
ed DR.. Finlay H. Mason for his
Front avenue property, according
to the ruling made by Judge Ken
nan Saturday overruling the mo
tion for a new trial. The case will
probably be appealed to the su
preme court.


The new symphony orchestra
Karl Riedelsberger conducting
made its debut at the Spokan,
theater yesterday Afternoon, sur
prising the large audience with thu
splendid showing.

yesterday afternoon at the Ode
